attempts.code_tried: If `y.asIdeal = ‚ä•`, then `y` is the generic point of the integral affine chart Spec(R) (via `genericPoint_eq_bot_of_affine`); open immersion `ùí∞.f i` sends generic-of-irreducible-source to generic-of-irreducible-target (`AlgebraicGeometry.genericPoint_eq_of_isOpenImmersion`); combined with `(ùí∞.f i).base y = Y.point`, forces `Y.point = genericPoint (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left`; but generic points are `IsMax` in the scheme's specialisation preorder (via `Scheme.le_iff_specializes.mpr` + `genericPoint_specializes`), so `Order.coheight Y.point = 0`, contradicting `Y.coheight = 1`.
attempts.insight: Generic-point + IsMax contradiction is qualitatively cheaper than topological-coheight transfer under open immersion. Dead-ends: `inferInstance` for `IrreducibleSpace ‚Ü•(ùí∞.X i)` does not auto-unfold through `openCover.X`; need explicit `show`-cast to `Spec R`. `Scheme.le_iff_specializes` takes its endpoints as IMPLICIT args ‚Äî must use `.mpr` with no explicit args. `AlgebraicGeometry.genericPoint_eq_bot_of_affine R` needs explicit R + `IsDomain ‚ÜëR` in scope. `IsIntegral (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar)` does NOT auto-cast from `IsIntegral (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left` ‚Äî manual `letI`-style bridge required.
